cases.evidence: Two identical scans retained 6 unique physical-column rows and stable IDs.
Suggestion id 397 refreshed in place from SSN to EMAIL.
Approved analyst decision and TOKENIZE override survived source-data change and rescan.
Rejected and manual decisions retained their IDs/statuses with one row per physical column.
Only the stale machine suggestion was removed; reviewed rows remained.
Excluded SSN suggestion was preserved and absent from EMAIL-scoped scan/results.
Focused rescan touched only scope_a and left scope_b byte-for-byte stable at the API contract level.
Concurrent statuses=200,200; 5 physical keys remained unique; neither response contained a duplicate-key error.
Injected database failure rolled the entire rescan back; clean rerun then discovered candidate_fail.
Results API and downloadable CSV projection both contain 6 current rows; database uniqueness audit found zero duplicate keys. UI parity is retained separately.
